name: eden-reference

services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:16.6-alpine
    container_name: eden-postgres
    restart: unless-stopped
    # Depending on blob-init lets `compose up --wait` interpret
    # blob-init's exit-0 as success rather than failure. Without
    # this dependency, `--wait` returns non-zero for any service
    # that exits during the wait window. Postgres does not actually
    # consume the blob volume; the dependency is a stack-level
    # invariant ("all infra is ready") rather than a data-flow one.
    depends_on:
      blob-init:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: ${POSTGRES_DB:-eden}
      POSTGRES_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER:-eden}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:?POSTGRES_PASSWORD must be set}
    ports:
      - "${POSTGRES_HOST_PORT:-5433}:5432"
    volumes:
      - eden-postgres-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER:-eden} -d ${POSTGRES_DB:-eden}"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 10
      start_period: 10s

  gitea:
    image: gitea/gitea:1.22.6-rootless
    container_name: eden-gitea
    restart: unless-stopped
    # See postgres above — depending on blob-init's successful
    # completion makes `compose up --wait` happy.
    depends_on:
      blob-init:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
    environment:
      GITEA__server__DOMAIN: localhost
      GITEA__server__ROOT_URL: http://localhost:${GITEA_HOST_PORT:-3001}/
      GITEA__server__HTTP_PORT: "3000"
      # SSH_PORT is the *advertised* port (shown in clone URLs); it must
      # match the host-side mapping so SSH clones work. SSH_LISTEN_PORT
      # is what Gitea binds inside the container, kept fixed at 2222
      # so the published mapping below stays valid even when a user
      # overrides GITEA_SSH_HOST_PORT to dodge a host collision.
      GITEA__server__SSH_PORT: "${GITEA_SSH_HOST_PORT:-2222}"
      GITEA__server__SSH_LISTEN_PORT: "2222"
      GITEA__database__DB_TYPE: sqlite3
      GITEA__database__PATH: /var/lib/gitea/data/gitea.db
      GITEA__security__INSTALL_LOCK: "true"
      GITEA__security__SECRET_KEY: ${GITEA_SECRET_KEY:?GITEA_SECRET_KEY must be set}
      GITEA__security__INTERNAL_TOKEN: ${GITEA_INTERNAL_TOKEN:?GITEA_INTERNAL_TOKEN must be set}
      GITEA__service__DISABLE_REGISTRATION: "true"
      GITEA__metrics__ENABLED: "false"
      GITEA__log__LEVEL: Warn
    ports:
      - "${GITEA_HOST_PORT:-3001}:3000"
      - "${GITEA_SSH_HOST_PORT:-2222}:2222"
    volumes:
      - eden-gitea-data:/var/lib/gitea
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "-q", "--spider", "http://localhost:3000/api/v1/version"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 20
      start_period: 30s

  blob-init:
    # One-shot service whose only job is to ensure eden-blob-data
    # exists at compose-up time. Compose does NOT create an unmounted
    # top-level volume — verified empirically — so without this service
    # the volume would not exist until 10b/10d's first real mounter
    # ran. 10a's contract is "volume exists" only; ownership and
    # permissions are 10d's responsibility.
    image: busybox:1.36.1
    container_name: eden-blob-init
    restart: "no"
    command: ["sh", "-c", "mkdir -p /var/lib/eden/blobs && echo blob-volume ready"]
    volumes:
      - eden-blob-data:/var/lib/eden/blobs

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------
  # 10b/10c: EDEN reference services
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------

  eden-repo-init:
    # Setup-time tool, NOT in any other service's depends_on. Invoked
    # by setup-experiment via `compose run --rm --no-deps`. The
    # `setup` profile keeps it out of plain `compose up`.
    profiles: ["setup"]
    image: eden-reference:dev
    build:
      context: ../..
      dockerfile: reference/compose/Dockerfile
    container_name: eden-repo-init
    restart: "no"
    command:
      - python
      - -m
      - eden_service_common.repo_init
      - --repo-path
      - /var/lib/eden/repo
    volumes:
      - eden-bare-repo:/var/lib/eden/repo

  task-store-server:
    image: eden-reference:dev
    build:
      context: ../..
      dockerfile: reference/compose/Dockerfile
    container_name: eden-task-store-server
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    command:
      - python
      - -m
      - eden_task_store_server
      - --store-url
      - ${EDEN_STORE_URL:?EDEN_STORE_URL must be set (run setup-experiment)}
      - --experiment-id
      - ${EDEN_EXPERIMENT_ID:?EDEN_EXPERIMENT_ID must be set (run setup-experiment)}
      - --experiment-config
      - /etc/eden/experiment-config.yaml
      - --shared-token
      - ${EDEN_SHARED_TOKEN:?EDEN_SHARED_TOKEN must be set (run setup-experiment)}
      - --host
      - 0.0.0.0
      - --port
      - "8080"
      - --log-level
      - info
    configs:
      - source: eden-experiment-config
        target: /etc/eden/experiment-config.yaml
    healthcheck:
      # Authenticated GET against an experiment-scoped endpoint
      # confirms both that the process is up and that auth wiring
      # works. CMD argv form (NOT CMD-SHELL) so Compose interpolates
      # `.env` values exactly once; no shell re-parses them, so
      # quotes / spaces / shell metacharacters in the token or
      # experiment id can't break or alter the command.
      test:
        - CMD
        - curl
        - -fsS
        - -H
        - "Authorization: Bearer ${EDEN_SHARED_TOKEN}"
        - -H
        - "X-Eden-Experiment-Id: ${EDEN_EXPERIMENT_ID}"
        - http://localhost:8080/v0/experiments/${EDEN_EXPERIMENT_ID}/events
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 20
      start_period: 10s

  orchestrator:
    image: eden-reference:dev
    build:
      context: ../..
      dockerfile: reference/compose/Dockerfile
    container_name: eden-orchestrator
    # `on-failure` (not `unless-stopped`) — the orchestrator exits 0
    # on quiescence, and `unless-stopped` would treat that as
    # something to recover from and re-run the loop in a tight
    # restart cycle. `on-failure` lets a clean exit stick.
    restart: "on-failure"
    depends_on:
      task-store-server:
        condition: service_healthy
    command:
      - python
      - -m
      - eden_orchestrator
      - --task-store-url
      - http://task-store-server:8080
      - --experiment-id
      - ${EDEN_EXPERIMENT_ID:?}
      - --shared-token
      - ${EDEN_SHARED_TOKEN:?}
      - --repo-path
      - /var/lib/eden/repo
      - --plan-tasks
      - ${EDEN_PLAN_TASKS:-3}
      # Compose-friendly quiescence: 30 iterations × 1s = 30s of
      # zero progress before the orchestrator declares the
      # experiment done. The default (3 × 0.1s = 0.3s) is tuned
      # for the in-process e2e tests where workers start
      # immediately; in Compose the worker hosts take longer to
      # bind and start polling.
      - --poll-interval
      - "1.0"
      - --max-quiescent-iterations
      - "30"
      - --log-level
      - info
    volumes:
      - eden-bare-repo:/var/lib/eden/repo

  planner-host:
    image: eden-reference:dev
    build:
      context: ../..
      dockerfile: reference/compose/Dockerfile
    container_name: eden-planner-host
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      task-store-server:
        condition: service_healthy
    command:
      - python
      - -m
      - eden_planner_host
      - --task-store-url
      - http://task-store-server:8080
      - --experiment-id
      - ${EDEN_EXPERIMENT_ID:?}
      - --shared-token
      - ${EDEN_SHARED_TOKEN:?}
      - --worker-id
      - planner-1
      - --base-commit-sha
      - ${EDEN_BASE_COMMIT_SHA:?EDEN_BASE_COMMIT_SHA must be set (run setup-experiment)}
      - --log-level
      - info

  implementer-host:
    image: eden-reference:dev
    build:
      context: ../..
      dockerfile: reference/compose/Dockerfile
    container_name: eden-implementer-host
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      task-store-server:
        condition: service_healthy
    command:
      - python
      - -m
      - eden_implementer_host
      - --task-store-url
      - http://task-store-server:8080
      - --experiment-id
      - ${EDEN_EXPERIMENT_ID:?}
      - --shared-token
      - ${EDEN_SHARED_TOKEN:?}
      - --worker-id
      - implementer-1
      - --repo-path
      - /var/lib/eden/repo
      - --log-level
      - info
    volumes:
      - eden-bare-repo:/var/lib/eden/repo

item.text: The route/template assumptions mostly line up so far. I’m checking the edge cases now: exact redirect targets, whether the CSRF regex is brittle against the actual HTML shape, and whether the compose termination assertions are making any stronger assumptions than the stack really guarantees.

1	"""Sign-in / sign-out routes.
     2	
     3	The Phase-9-chunk-1 sign-in is a no-questions form: any GET to
     4	``/signin`` shows a button labeled "Continue as <worker-id>", a
     5	POST issues a fresh session cookie, and ``/signout`` clears it.
     6	Real per-user auth lives in Milestone 3.
     7	"""
     8	
     9	from __future__ import annotations
    10	
    11	from fastapi import APIRouter, Request
    12	from fastapi.responses import HTMLResponse, RedirectResponse
    13	
    14	from ..sessions import SESSION_COOKIE_NAME, Session, new_csrf_token
    15	from ._helpers import write_session_cookie
    16	
    17	router = APIRouter()
    18	
    19	
    20	@router.get("/signin", response_class=HTMLResponse)
    21	async def signin_form(request: Request) -> HTMLResponse:
    22	    return request.app.state.templates.TemplateResponse(
    23	        request,
    24	        "signin.html",
    25	        {"worker_id": request.app.state.worker_id},
    26	    )
    27	
    28	
    29	@router.post("/signin", response_model=None)
    30	async def signin_submit(request: Request) -> RedirectResponse:
    31	    session = Session(
    32	        worker_id=request.app.state.worker_id,
    33	        csrf=new_csrf_token(),
    34	    )
    35	    encoded = request.app.state.session_codec.encode(session)
    36	    response = RedirectResponse(url="/", status_code=303)
    37	    write_session_cookie(
    38	        response,
    39	        encoded=encoded,
    40	        secure=request.app.state.secure_cookies,
    41	    )
    42	    return response
    43	
    44	
    45	@router.post("/signout", response_model=None)
    46	async def signout(request: Request) -> RedirectResponse:
    47	    response = RedirectResponse(url="/signin", status_code=303)
    48	    response.delete_cookie(SESSION_COOKIE_NAME, path="/")
    49	    return response

60	def _list_recent_trials(store: Any, *, limit: int = 20) -> list[Any]:

   143	    """Append one empty proposal row to the draft form.
   144	
   145	    Two transports, same end state:
   146	
   147	    - With JS, htmx posts here with ``HX-Request: true``. We respond
   148	      with the rendered ``_proposal_row.html`` fragment for the new
   149	      row only; htmx swaps it as ``beforeend`` of ``#proposal-rows``
   150	      so the user's existing input is untouched. Redirect/error
   151	      branches send ``HX-Redirect`` on a 204 so htmx does a full
   152	      client-side navigation rather than swapping a redirected page
   153	      into the rows container.

     2	set -euo pipefail
     3	
     4	# Phase 10e end-to-end smoke for the EDEN reference Compose stack.
     5	# Beyond compose-smoke (headless 3-trial loop) and
     6	# compose-smoke-subprocess (subprocess-mode loop), this drill exercises:
     7	#
     8	#   1. Web UI planner walkthrough (sign-in, claim, submit)
     9	#   2. Admin-reclaim drill (claim, do-not-submit, admin-reclaim)
    10	#   3. End-state assertions adjusted for EDEN_PLAN_TASKS=4
    11	#   4. Termination drill (compose stop --timeout 10; verify no SIGKILL)
    12	#
    13	# See docs/plans/eden-phase-10e-compose-e2e.md for the design rationale,
    14	# in particular §B (race-free staged bring-up).
    15	
    16	# Tooling preflight.
    17	for tool in docker jq curl python3; do
    18	    command -v "$tool" >/dev/null || {
    19	        echo "e2e.sh requires '$tool' on PATH" >&2
    20	        exit 2
    21	    }
    22	done
    23	docker compose version >/dev/null || {
    24	    echo "e2e.sh requires the 'docker compose' v2 plugin" >&2
    25	    exit 2
    26	}
    27	python3 -c 'import httpx' 2>/dev/null || {
    28	    echo "e2e.sh requires 'httpx' to be importable from python3" >&2
    29	    echo "  install with: pip install 'httpx>=0.27,<1'" >&2
    30	    exit 2
    31	}
    32	
    33	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"

    37	cd "$COMPOSE_DIR"

    39	ENV_FILE="$(mktemp)"

    46	STAGE1_SERVICES=(

   164	    echo "$EVENTS_JSON" \
   165	        | jq '(.events // .) | [.[] | select(
   166	              .type == "task.completed"
   167	              and (.data.task_id | startswith("plan-"))
   168	            )] | length'
   169	)"
   170	test "$PLAN_COMPLETED" -ge 4 || {
   171	    echo "expected >= 4 plan-task.completed events; got $PLAN_COMPLETED" >&2
   172	    exit 1
   173	}
   174	RECLAIMED_OPERATOR="$(
   175	    echo "$EVENTS_JSON" \
   176	        | jq '(.events // .) | [.[] | select(
   177	              .type == "task.reclaimed"
   178	              and .data.cause == "operator"
   179	            )] | length'
   180	)"
   181	test "$RECLAIMED_OPERATOR" -ge 1 || {
   182	    echo "expected >= 1 task.reclaimed (cause=operator) event; got $RECLAIMED_OPERATOR" >&2
   183	    exit 1
   184	}
   185	
   186	echo "--- termination drill: stop with 10s budget ---"
   187	docker compose -f compose.yaml --env-file "$ENV_FILE" stop --timeout 10
   188	
   189	# Iterate every container compose still knows about. With
   190	# eden-repo-init removed by `compose run --rm`, this naturally covers
   191	# postgres, blob-init, task-store-server, orchestrator, planner-host,
   192	# implementer-host, evaluator-host, and web-ui. (Gitea was never
   193	# brought up so it does not appear here — codex round-1 fix.)
   194	#
   195	# Use process substitution + while-read instead of `mapfile` so this
   196	# works under bash 3.2 (macOS default) and CI's bash 5+.
   197	CONTAINERS=()
   198	while IFS= read -r name; do
   199	    [[ -n "$name" ]] && CONTAINERS+=("$name")
   200	done < <(
   201	    docker compose -f compose.yaml --env-file "$ENV_FILE" \
   202	        ps -a --format '{{.Name}}'
   203	)
   204	test "${#CONTAINERS[@]}" -gt 0 || {
   205	    echo "compose ps -a returned no containers; nothing to assert" >&2
   206	    exit 1
   207	}
   208	
   209	for name in "${CONTAINERS[@]}"; do
   210	    status="$(docker inspect --format '{{.State.Status}}' "$name")"
   211	    code="$(docker inspect --format '{{.State.ExitCode}}' "$name")"
   212	    test "$status" = "exited" || {
   213	        echo "$name still in status $status after stop --timeout 10" >&2
   214	        exit 1
   215	    }
   216	    test "$code" != "137" || {
   217	        echo "$name was SIGKILLed (exit code 137)" >&2
   218	        docker compose -f compose.yaml --env-file "$ENV_FILE" \
   219	            logs --tail 30 "$name" >&2
   220	        exit 1
   221	    }
   222	done
   223	
   224	echo "PASS"

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""Phase 10e end-to-end Compose drill — python driver.
     3	
     4	Drives a single planner walkthrough and a single admin-reclaim drill
     5	against the live web-ui container, then exits zero on success or
     6	non-zero with a diagnostic on failure.
     7	
     8	Run from ``e2e.sh``; expects these env vars:
     9	
    10	- ``EDEN_E2E_WEB_UI_URL`` — base URL of the web-ui (e.g.
    11	  ``http://localhost:8090``)
    12	- ``EDEN_BASE_COMMIT_SHA`` — 40-hex SHA to use as ``parent_commits``
    13	  on the planner submit (read from setup-experiment's ``.env``)
    14	
    15	The flow is documented in
    16	``docs/plans/eden-phase-10e-compose-e2e.md`` §C / §D.
    17	"""
    18	
    19	from __future__ import annotations
    20	
    21	import os
    22	import re
    23	import sys
    24	import time
    25	from urllib.parse import urlencode
    26	
    27	import httpx
    28	
    29	_CSRF_RE = re.compile(r'name="csrf_token"\s+value="([^"]+)"')
    30	
    31	
    32	def _fail(msg: str, *, response: httpx.Response | None = None) -> None:
    33	    """Print a diagnostic to stderr and exit non-zero."""
    34	    print(f"e2e_drive: {msg}", file=sys.stderr)
    35	    if response is not None:
    36	        print(f"  status: {response.status_code}", file=sys.stderr)
    37	        body = response.text
    38	        excerpt = body if len(body) <= 800 else body[:800] + "…"
    39	        print(f"  body: {excerpt}", file=sys.stderr)
    40	    sys.exit(1)
    41	
    42	
    43	def _scrape_csrf(html: str, *, where: str) -> str:
    44	    m = _CSRF_RE.search(html)
    45	    if m is None:
    46	        _fail(f"could not find csrf_token in {where} response body")
    47	        # _fail exits; this return is unreachable but appeases the type checker
    48	        return ""
    49	    return m.group(1)
    50	
    51	
    52	def _form_post(
    53	    ui: httpx.Client, path: str, fields: list[tuple[str, str]]
    54	) -> httpx.Response:
    55	    return ui.post(
    56	        path,
    57	        content=urlencode(fields),
    58	        headers={"content-type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded"},
    59	        follow_redirects=False,
    60	    )
    61	
    62	
    63	def _wait_for_seeded_tasks(
    64	    ui: httpx.Client, ids: tuple[str, ...], deadline_s: float
    65	) -> None:
    66	    """Poll ``/planner/`` until all ``ids`` are visible.
    67	
    68	    The orchestrator has no compose healthcheck so ``compose up --wait``
    69	    can return before seeding completes. Polling avoids both an
    70	    arbitrary sleep and a race against slow startup.
    71	    """
    72	    end = time.monotonic() + deadline_s
    73	    last: httpx.Response | None = None
    74	    while time.monotonic() < end:
    75	        resp = ui.get("/planner/")
    76	        last = resp
    77	        if resp.status_code == 200 and all(tid in resp.text for tid in ids):
    78	            return
    79	        time.sleep(0.5)
    80	    _fail(
    81	        f"orchestrator did not seed all tasks {ids} within "
    82	        f"{deadline_s:.0f}s",
    83	        response=last,
    84	    )
    85	
    86	
    87	def _planner_walkthrough(
    88	    ui: httpx.Client, task_id: str, base_commit_sha: str
    89	) -> None:
    90	    """Claim → draft → submit one plan task.
    91	
    92	    The submit returns 200 with the rendered ``planner_submitted.html``
    93	    template (NOT a 303 — see chunk-9b
    94	    ``reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/routes/planner.py``).
    95	    """
    96	    # GET /planner/ to scrape CSRF for the claim form.
    97	    resp = ui.get("/planner/")
    98	    if resp.status_code != 200:
    99	        _fail("GET /planner/ failed", response=resp)
   100	    csrf = _scrape_csrf(resp.text, where="GET /planner/")
   101	
   102	    # Claim.
   103	    resp = _form_post(ui, f"/planner/{task_id}/claim", [("csrf_token", csrf)])
   104	    if resp.status_code != 303:
   105	        _fail(f"claim {task_id} did not 303", response=resp)
   106	
   107	    # Draft form. Re-scrape CSRF — the draft renders its own form.
   108	    resp = ui.get(f"/planner/{task_id}/draft")
   109	    if resp.status_code != 200:
   110	        _fail(f"GET /planner/{task_id}/draft failed", response=resp)
   111	    csrf = _scrape_csrf(resp.text, where=f"GET /planner/{task_id}/draft")
   112	
   113	    # Submit a single proposal row.
   114	    fields = [
   115	        ("csrf_token", csrf),
   116	        ("status", "success"),
   117	        ("slug", "e2e-feat"),
   118	        ("priority", "1.0"),
   119	        ("parent_commits", base_commit_sha),
   120	        ("rationale", "## why\n\nphase 10e end-to-end drill.\n"),
   121	    ]
   122	    resp = _form_post(ui, f"/planner/{task_id}/submit", fields)
   123	    if resp.status_code != 200:
   124	        _fail(f"submit {task_id} returned non-200", response=resp)
   125	    if "submitted" not in resp.text.lower():
   126	        _fail(
   127	            f"submit {task_id} 200 but body lacks 'submitted'", response=resp
   128	        )
   129	
   130	
   131	def _admin_reclaim_drill(ui: httpx.Client, task_id: str) -> None:
   132	    """Claim a plan task via web UI, do not submit, then admin-reclaim it.
   133	
   134	    The admin reclaim redirect URL has a TRAILING SLASH before the
   135	    query (``/admin/tasks/<id>/?reclaimed=ok``) — see chunk-9e
   136	    ``reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/routes/admin.py``.
   137	    """
   138	    # Claim.
   139	    resp = ui.get("/planner/")
   140	    if resp.status_code != 200:
   141	        _fail("GET /planner/ for admin drill failed", response=resp)
   142	    csrf = _scrape_csrf(resp.text, where="GET /planner/ (admin drill)")
   143	    resp = _form_post(ui, f"/planner/{task_id}/claim", [("csrf_token", csrf)])
   144	    if resp.status_code != 303:
   145	        _fail(
   146	            f"admin-drill claim {task_id} did not 303", response=resp
   147	        )
   148	
   149	    # Confirm it shows up in the admin claimed list.
   150	    resp = ui.get("/admin/tasks/?state=claimed")
   151	    if resp.status_code != 200:
   152	        _fail("GET /admin/tasks/?state=claimed failed", response=resp)
   153	    if task_id not in resp.text:
   154	        _fail(
   155	            f"{task_id} not visible in /admin/tasks/?state=claimed",
   156	            response=resp,
   157	        )
   158	
   159	    # Re-scrape CSRF from the task-detail page (its own form render).
   160	    resp = ui.get(f"/admin/tasks/{task_id}/")
   161	    if resp.status_code != 200:
   162	        _fail(f"GET /admin/tasks/{task_id}/ failed", response=resp)
   163	    csrf = _scrape_csrf(resp.text, where=f"GET /admin/tasks/{task_id}/")
   164	
   165	    # Reclaim.
   166	    resp = _form_post(
   167	        ui, f"/admin/tasks/{task_id}/reclaim", [("csrf_token", csrf)]
   168	    )
   169	    if resp.status_code != 303:
   170	        _fail(
   171	            f"admin reclaim {task_id} did not 303", response=resp
   172	        )
   173	    expected = f"/admin/tasks/{task_id}/?reclaimed=ok"
   174	    location = resp.headers.get("location", "")
   175	    if location != expected:
   176	        _fail(
   177	            f"admin reclaim {task_id} redirected to {location!r}; "
   178	            f"expected {expected!r}",
   179	            response=resp,
   180	        )
   181	
   182	
   183	def main() -> int:
   184	    """Drive the planner walkthrough and admin-reclaim drill end-to-end."""
   185	    web_url = os.environ.get("EDEN_E2E_WEB_UI_URL")
   186	    base_sha = os.environ.get("EDEN_BASE_COMMIT_SHA")
   187	    if not web_url:
   188	        _fail("EDEN_E2E_WEB_UI_URL not set in environment")
   189	    if not base_sha:
   190	        _fail("EDEN_BASE_COMMIT_SHA not set in environment")
   191	    # _fail exits non-zero, but the type checker doesn't know that;
   192	    # narrow with two separate asserts (ruff PT018 — one assertion per
   193	    # condition).
   194	    assert web_url is not None  # noqa: S101 — type narrowing only
   195	    assert base_sha is not None  # noqa: S101 — type narrowing only
   196	
   197	    seeded_ids = tuple(f"plan-{i:04d}" for i in range(1, 5))
   198	    submit_id = "plan-0001"
   199	    reclaim_id = "plan-0002"
   200	
   201	    print(f"e2e_drive: connecting to {web_url}", flush=True)
   202	    with httpx.Client(base_url=web_url, timeout=15.0) as ui:
   203	        # Sign in (anonymous stub) BEFORE polling — admin/planner pages
   204	        # redirect to /signin without a session, so an unauthenticated
   205	        # poll loop would just see 303s.
   206	        resp = ui.post("/signin", follow_redirects=False)
   207	        if resp.status_code != 303:
   208	            _fail("POST /signin did not 303", response=resp)
   209	
   210	        # Wait for the orchestrator's seed to land. Polls /planner/ —
   211	        # both /planner/ and /admin/tasks/ are auth-gated, so this only
   212	        # works after sign-in.
   213	        _wait_for_seeded_tasks(ui, seeded_ids, deadline_s=30.0)
   214	        print(
   215	            f"e2e_drive: all {len(seeded_ids)} seeded plan tasks visible",
   216	            flush=True,
   217	        )
   218	
   219	        _planner_walkthrough(ui, submit_id, base_commit_sha=base_sha)
   220	        print(
   221	            f"e2e_drive: planner walkthrough OK ({submit_id} submitted)",
   222	            flush=True,
   223	        )
   224	
   225	        _admin_reclaim_drill(ui, reclaim_id)
   226	        print(
   227	            f"e2e_drive: admin-reclaim drill OK ({reclaim_id} reclaimed)",
   228	            flush=True,
   229	        )
   230	
   231	    print("e2e_drive: PASS", flush=True)
   232	    return 0
   233	
   234	
   235	if __name__ == "__main__":
   236	    raise SystemExit(main())

docs/roadmap.md:160:- **8a — complete.** [`spec/v0/07-wire-protocol.md`](../spec/v0/07-wire-protocol.md) pins the HTTP binding for chapters 4, 5, 6 §3.4, and 8 §§1.1–2.1. The `eden-wire` package ships a FastAPI `make_app(store)` server plus an httpx-backed `StoreClient` that satisfies the same `Store` Protocol. `Store.integrate_trial` is now same-value idempotent so HTTP retries are safe; the `Integrator` distinguishes different-SHA divergence (`AtomicityViolation`) from compensable synchronous rejections; `StoreClient` resolves transport-indeterminate failures via read-back. Long-poll and non-blocking polling both bind the §2.1 `subscribe` operation. Errors round-trip as RFC 7807 problem+json with a closed `eden://error/<name>` vocabulary (chapter §7).
docs/roadmap.md:201:- **10e — complete.** New `compose-e2e` CI job runs [`reference/compose/healthcheck/e2e.sh`](../reference/compose/healthcheck/e2e.sh), which exercises the stack-shape coverage missing from `compose-smoke` / `compose-smoke-subprocess`: a Web UI planner walkthrough (sign-in → claim → submit), an admin-reclaim drill (claim → admin-reclaim with a `task.reclaimed` `cause=operator` event), and a termination drill (`compose stop --timeout 10`; every container in `compose ps -a` ends `exited` with `ExitCode != 137` — the SIGKILL marker — chosen over `== 0` because external images don't always install graceful SIGTERM handlers and 143 from a default-handler is also a clean termination). Bring-up is **staged** to defeat the planner-host-claim race: stage 1 omits both planner-host and gitea (gitea is the deferred 10d follow-up; not consumed here), the python driver in [`reference/compose/healthcheck/e2e_drive.py`](../reference/compose/healthcheck/e2e_drive.py) runs the UI walkthroughs against the seeded-but-unclaimed plan tasks, then stage 2 brings planner-host online so the experiment proceeds to quiescence (the orchestrator's `--max-quiescent-iterations` × `--poll-interval` = 30s of zero progress before exit 0). `EDEN_PLAN_TASKS=4` is overridden in the staged `.env` (vs smoke's default 3) so plan-0001 / plan-0002 carry the UI walkthrough + admin-reclaim drill while plan-0003 / plan-0004 (and the reclaimed plan-0002) flow through the headless host. The python driver polls `/planner/` for all 4 seeded IDs to land before claiming, because the orchestrator has no compose healthcheck so `compose up --wait` returns as soon as the container is `running` — which can precede the seed completing. Sign-in must happen **before** the poll because both `/planner/` and `/admin/tasks/` redirect unauthed sessions to `/signin` (a polling loop without sign-in would just see 303s). Driver assertions match the actual route shapes (codex round-1 corrections from the plan): planner submit returns 200 with `planner_submitted.html` (NOT 303); admin reclaim redirects to `/admin/tasks/<id>/?reclaimed=ok` *with the trailing slash*; orchestrator-seeded plan tasks are `plan-{i:04d}` not bare-int. **Roadmap delta:** container isolation of the `*_command` subprocess (docker-in-docker, experiment-specific image build, auth-secret mounting) and Gitea-as-the-workers'-actual-git-remote remain deferred per 10d's plan. Branch protection is **not** updated to require `compose-e2e` in this chunk — same posture 10c / 10d took for newly-added compose jobs (let it run cleanly for a few iterations on `main` first).

### I. Teardown

`e2e.sh`'s `cleanup()` trap runs `docker compose down -v` on EXIT,
exactly as smoke.sh does. The termination drill's `compose stop` does
NOT call `down -v` itself — that would lose the volumes before the
drill verifies anything. `down -v` is the trap's responsibility;
`stop` is the drill's.

### J. CI wiring

Add `compose-e2e` to `.github/workflows/ci.yml` mirroring
`compose-smoke-subprocess`:

```yaml
compose-e2e:
  name: compose-e2e
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  timeout-minutes: 20  # 5 more than smoke jobs to absorb the drill
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - name: Verify docker compose + jq + python3 are available
      run: |
        docker compose version
        jq --version
        python3 --version
    - name: Install python driver deps
      run: pip install 'httpx>=0.27,<1'
    - name: Run compose e2e
      working-directory: reference/compose
      run: bash healthcheck/e2e.sh
```

**httpx pin** is `>=0.27,<1` (codex round-0 fix), matching the
workspace's existing pin in
[`eden-wire/pyproject.toml:8`](../../reference/packages/eden-wire/pyproject.toml)
and
[`_common/pyproject.toml:8`](../../reference/services/_common/pyproject.toml).
A bare `pip install` is lighter than wiring up the full `uv sync` —
the CI job only needs the python driver to talk HTTP, no workspace
imports.

1	# AGENTS.md
     2	
     3	This file provides guidance to AI agents working with this repository.
     4	
     5	## What this repo is
     6	
     7	EDEN is a **protocol** for directed-code-evolution orchestration, not a single product. The repo contains three distinct kinds of artifact, each with different change discipline:
     8	
     9	| Layer | Path | Authoritative? | Change discipline |
    10	|---|---|---|---|
    11	| Protocol specification | [`spec/`](spec/) | Yes — the source of truth | RFC-style: versioned, normative (MUST/SHOULD/MAY), carefully reviewed |
    12	| Reference implementation | [`reference/`](reference/) | No — *one* valid impl | Normal code-review discipline |
    13	| Conformance suite | [`conformance/`](conformance/) | Normative for tests | Black-box; must be implementation-agnostic |
    14	
    15	Non-normative human docs live in [`docs/`](docs/).
    16	
    17	## Current phase
    18	
    19	**Phase 10 chunk 10e complete.** A new `compose-e2e` CI job runs [`reference/compose/healthcheck/e2e.sh`](reference/compose/healthcheck/e2e.sh) — the stack-shape coverage missing from `compose-smoke` / `compose-smoke-subprocess`: a Web UI planner walkthrough (sign-in → claim → submit), an admin-reclaim drill (claim → admin-reclaim with a `task.reclaimed` `cause=operator` event in the log), and a termination drill (`compose stop --timeout 10`; every container in `compose ps -a` ends in `exited` with `ExitCode != 137`). The `!= 137` SIGKILL guard is deliberately weaker than `== 0`: external images don't always install graceful SIGTERM handlers, and 143 from a default-handler is also a clean termination — what we actually care about is that no container needed SIGKILL. Bring-up is **staged** to defeat the planner-host-claim race that the round-0 codex review flagged: stage 1 omits both planner-host AND gitea (gitea's 30s healthcheck `start_period` would race against the orchestrator's 30s quiescence budget; gitea is the deferred 10d follow-up sub-chunk and isn't consumed by 10e). The python driver in [`reference/compose/healthcheck/e2e_drive.py`](reference/compose/healthcheck/e2e_drive.py) runs the UI walkthroughs against the seeded-but-unclaimed plan tasks, then stage 2 brings planner-host online so the experiment proceeds to quiescence and the orchestrator exits 0. `EDEN_PLAN_TASKS=4` is overridden in the staged `.env` (vs smoke's default 3) so plan-0001 / plan-0002 carry the UI walkthrough + admin-reclaim drill while plan-0003 / plan-0004 (and the reclaimed plan-0002) flow through the headless host. The python driver polls `/planner/` for all 4 seeded IDs to land before claiming — the orchestrator has no compose healthcheck so `compose up --wait` returns as soon as the container is `running`, which can precede the seed completing. The poll happens **after** sign-in, not before: `/planner/` and `/admin/tasks/` redirect unauthed sessions to `/signin`, so an unauthenticated poll loop would just see 303s. Driver assertions match the actual route shapes: planner submit returns 200 with `planner_submitted.html` (NOT 303); admin reclaim redirects to `/admin/tasks/<id>/?reclaimed=ok` *with the trailing slash*; orchestrator-seeded plan tasks are `plan-{i:04d}` not bare-int. Termination drill iterates the full `compose ps -a` output (not eden-* only) — covers postgres, blob-init, task-store-server, orchestrator, planner-host, implementer-host, evaluator-host, and web-ui (gitea was never started in stage 1 so it doesn't appear; eden-repo-init ran via `compose run --rm` and is gone). Local mac compatibility: the container loop uses portable `while IFS= read -r … done < <(…)` instead of bash 4+'s `mapfile` builtin so the script runs under macOS's default bash 3.2. New CI job is **not** required by branch protection in this chunk (same posture 10c / 10d took for newly-added compose jobs).
    20	
    21	**Phase 10 chunk 10d complete.** Each of the planner / implementer / evaluator hosts gains a new `--mode subprocess` that invokes a user-supplied command from the experiment-config YAML (`plan_command` / `implement_command` / `evaluate_command`) instead of the deterministic Phase-8b scripted profile. The subprocess invocation is via `shell=True` so user expressions like `python3 ${EDEN_EXPERIMENT_DIR}/plan.py` expand correctly; the host supplies `EDEN_EXPERIMENT_DIR`, `EDEN_TASK_JSON`, `EDEN_OUTPUT`, and (for implement/eval) `EDEN_WORKTREE`. Planner subprocess is **long-running** with a JSON-line `ready` / `plan` / `proposal` / `plan-done` protocol over stdin/stdout (so user code can hold accumulating LLM context across plan tasks); implementer + evaluator subprocesses are **per-task short-lived** with cwd set to a per-task git worktree, and read/write outcome JSON via the `EDEN_OUTPUT` path. The implementer flow honors [`spec/v0/03-roles.md`](spec/v0/03-roles.md) §3.2 step 1 — `Store.create_trial(status="starting")` runs **before** any repository write, mirroring the chunk-9c web-ui implementer ordering. Worktrees are host-private under `<worktrees-dir>/<container_hostname>/<task_id>/`; startup-time cleanup uses path-scoped `git worktree remove --force` (never repo-global `git worktree prune`), so cross-host races don't exist by construction. The chapter-3 submission shapes carry no free-form `description` field; worker-side failure context (subprocess timeout, malformed outcome JSON, etc.) goes to the host log only. The protocol is documented as a non-normative **reference binding** at [`spec/v0/reference-bindings/worker-host-subprocess.md`](spec/v0/reference-bindings/worker-host-subprocess.md) — a conforming alternative host can either match the shape (drop-in compatibility with the fixture experiment) or pick a different mechanism. Three new fixture scripts ([`tests/fixtures/experiment/plan.py`](tests/fixtures/experiment/plan.py), [`implement.py`](tests/fixtures/experiment/implement.py), [`eval.py`](tests/fixtures/experiment/eval.py)) exercise the protocol deterministically without LLM secrets so the new `compose-smoke-subprocess` CI job can run end-to-end alongside the original `compose-smoke`. The fixture's `*_command` config keys gain `${EDEN_EXPERIMENT_DIR}/...` prefixes (single source of truth: the experiment YAML, not a CLI flag). Compose plumbing is a non-invasive overlay [`reference/compose/compose.subprocess.yaml`](reference/compose/compose.subprocess.yaml) layered via `compose -f compose.yaml -f compose.subprocess.yaml`; setup-experiment grows `--experiment-dir` (defaults to the directory containing the config's `.eden`) and writes `EDEN_EXPERIMENT_DIR_HOST` into `.env` for the bind-mount. Container isolation of the user's command (docker-in-docker, experiment-specific image build, auth-secret mounting) is **deferred to a 10d follow-up sub-chunk**; the subprocess.Popen call is the wrappable layer, and the protocol stays unchanged when wrapped. Branch protection is not updated to require `compose-smoke-subprocess` in this chunk (same posture 10a / 10b+10c took for newly-added jobs).
    22	
    23	**Phase 10 chunks 10b + 10c complete.** Six EDEN reference services are now dockerized and wired into the Compose stack at [`reference/compose/`](reference/compose/), and a new [`reference/scripts/setup-experiment/setup-experiment.sh`](reference/scripts/setup-experiment/setup-experiment.sh) bootstraps a runnable stack from an experiment-config YAML in one shot. After `bash setup-experiment.sh <config.yaml> --experiment-id <id> && docker compose --env-file .env up -d --wait`, the fixture experiment runs to completion in Compose: orchestrator dispatches three plan tasks → planner-host claims/submits → orchestrator integrates → 3 trials succeed and integrate, with the orchestrator exiting 0 on quiescence (`restart: "on-failure"` so exit-0 sticks; tuned to `--poll-interval 1.0 --max-quiescent-iterations 30` for Compose worker-startup latency). All seven services (six EDEN services + setup-time `eden-repo-init`) share **one** `eden-reference:dev` image (multi-stage build with `uv sync --frozen --no-dev --all-packages` — the `--all-packages` flag is load-bearing because the workspace root pins `tool.uv.package = false` and lists the `eden-*` workspace members only in the `dev` group, which `--no-dev` would otherwise skip). The roadmap delta documenting the per-service-image deviation, the deferral of the LLM-implementer experiment-specific image to 10d, the deferral of control-plane registration to Phase 12, and the deferral of Gitea-as-the-workers'-actual-git-remote to a follow-up sub-chunk after 10d is recorded in the plan and reflected in the chunk's roadmap update. New `eden-storage.PostgresStore` ([`reference/packages/eden-storage/src/eden_storage/postgres.py`](reference/packages/eden-storage/src/eden_storage/postgres.py)) is the third backend conforming to the same `Store` Protocol as `InMemoryStore`/`SqliteStore`; backed by `psycopg[binary]>=3.2,<4` with `autocommit=True` + explicit per-op `BEGIN ISOLATION LEVEL SERIALIZABLE READ WRITE` (psycopg's auto-begin would error `ActiveSqlTransaction` on `BEGIN ISOLATION LEVEL`). Schema is byte-for-byte parallel to SQLite (`data text NOT NULL`, not `jsonb` — keeps the read path identical and defers indexing concerns). The new `python-test-postgres` CI job spins up `postgres:16.6-alpine` as a service container and runs the parametrized backend tests against it; the existing `python-test` job continues to run with `EDEN_TEST_POSTGRES_DSN` unset (so the postgres rows skip), keeping it fast. `task-store-server` gains a URL-based `--store-url` flag (`:memory:` / `sqlite:///<path>` / `postgresql://…` / bare-path-for-compat); the deprecated `--db-path` alias survives for one phase. Bare-repo bootstrap is owned **synchronously** by setup-experiment (one canonical command shape: `docker compose build eden-repo-init && docker compose run --rm --no-deps eden-repo-init` — `--no-deps` keeps future dependency edits from accidentally starting Postgres/Gitea just to seed). The seed SHA is captured from stdout and replaced into a placeholder line in `.env` (zero-padded sentinel) — *replaced*, not appended, so `grep -E '^EDEN_BASE_COMMIT_SHA='` returns one definition. The `eden-repo-init` service has `profiles: ["setup"]` so it's invisible to plain `compose up`. Volume permissions are pre-created in the Dockerfile via `RUN mkdir -p /var/lib/eden/{repo,artifacts} && chown -R eden:eden /var/lib/eden` — without this the named volume inherits root ownership from the volume driver and the eden user can't write. Healthchecks for `task-store-server` and `web-ui` use compose-load-time `${VAR}` interpolation (single `$`), not container-shell `$${VAR}`, because the env-file vars aren't propagated into the container's shell environment unless declared under `environment:`; baking the values into the literal command at compose-load is simpler. The web-ui mount is **read-write**, not read-only, because passing `--repo-path` activates the entire implementer module (chunk 9c) which writes refs on submit — read-only would 500. The redundancy with `implementer-host` is intentional (operators pick which they prefer; both race for claim atomicity via `Store.claim` chapter 04 §3). New `web-ui /healthz` endpoint is unauthenticated by design (compose healthchecks must work before any user signs in; reveals only "process is up"). Extended [`reference/compose/healthcheck/smoke.sh`](reference/compose/healthcheck/smoke.sh) (the `compose-smoke` CI job's invariant) now runs setup-experiment, asserts the seeded ref *before* `compose up` via a one-shot probe that reuses the already-built `eden-reference:dev` image (avoiding a floating `latest` tag), waits up to 180s for the orchestrator to exit 0 on quiescence, and verifies the final task-store state on the wire endpoint: ≥3 `trial.integrated` events (full pipeline), ≥9 `task.completed` events (3 plan + 3 implement + 3 evaluate), and ≥3 `task.completed` events whose `task_id` starts with `plan-` (each seeded plan task reached a terminal state). Branch protection is **not** updated to require `python-test-postgres` in this chunk — that lands in a follow-up after the job has a few clean runs (same posture 10a took for `compose-smoke`).
    24	
    25	**Phase 9 complete (chunk 9e shipped).** The Web UI service gains an `/admin/*` surface that closes Phase 9: read-only dashboards over the experiment's tasks (filterable by `kind`/`state`, with claim-age + claim-expired badges), trials (filterable by `status`, with an orphaned-starting-trial badge for trials whose owning implement task went terminal without driving the trial atomically), and the event log (capped at a configurable limit; natural-index-before-slice discipline so an event's rendered position is stable across filter / limit / reverse operations); a per-task detail page that exposes operator `reclaim` via `Store.reclaim(task_id, "operator")` per [`spec/v0/04-task-protocol.md`](spec/v0/04-task-protocol.md) §5.1, with separate "reclaim" / "force-reclaim (replays work)" UI variants gating the `claimed` vs `submitted` cases and a closed allowlist of `?reclaimed=ok` / `?error=…` banner outcomes (no arbitrary text echo); a per-trial detail page with a related-events filter that unions `event.data.trial_id == trial_id` plus task-id matches for the implement task that produced this trial and any evaluate task that references it, ordered by replay-index position (the `event_id` factory is pluggable per [`reference/packages/eden-storage/src/eden_storage/_base.py`](reference/packages/eden-storage/src/eden_storage/_base.py) and is not a reliable ordering contract); and a `work/*` ref garbage-collection page (when `--repo-path` is set) that classifies each ref by **exact `trial.branch` equality** — not by parsing the ref name — and offers CAS-guarded `repo.delete_ref(ref_name, expected_old_sha=…)` deletion only for terminal-and-handled trials whose `commit_sha` matches the live ref SHA, plus orphan-ref deletion when no trial owns the branch. The route layer never trusts the form's hidden `expected_old_sha`; it does its own fresh `list_refs` lookup at POST time and uses *that* SHA. The auth-first POST discipline matches existing modules (planner / implementer / evaluator): `get_session(request)` runs before `csrf_ok`, so an unauthenticated POST redirects to `/signin` (303) and an authenticated POST with a missing/wrong CSRF token returns 403. The `make_app` factory gains an `@app.exception_handler(eden_storage.errors.NotFound)` that translates raised storage/wire `NotFound` into the same `_error.html` 404 page the existing HTTP-404 handler renders (the prior handler only caught HTTP 404 *responses*, not raised `NotFound` exceptions). [`routes/admin.py`](reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/routes/admin.py) is mounted unconditionally; the work-refs sub-page handles the `repo=None` case in-template. Tests cover per-route validation (`test_admin_routes.py`, 33 tests), cross-request flows including planner-claim → admin-reclaim round trip + admin trial-detail rendering for an evaluator-seeded trial (`test_admin_flow.py`, 3 tests), security invariants including unauthenticated-POST-redirects-before-CSRF, error/reclaimed banner allowlist, ref-name regex rejection of every traversal/escape variant including `refs/heads/trial/*` / `HEAD` / shell-injection-shaped names with `subprocess.run` asserted uncalled, server-side `expected_old_sha` source (the form-hidden field is overridden by the live SHA), and the `javascript:` URI scheme allowlist on trial detail (`test_admin_security.py`, 21 tests), partial-write recovery for the four failure modes — `IllegalTransition` on terminal reclaim, transport-shaped `Exception` from the store with no auto-retry, GET-time/POST-time eligibility divergence, and CAS-miss → `?error=ref-changed` (`test_admin_partial_write.py`, 5 tests), and a `pytest.mark.e2e` real-subprocess test that drives planner-claim + admin-reclaim through real HTTP and verifies the resulting `task.reclaimed` event with `cause=operator` via a separate `StoreClient` (`test_admin_e2e.py`).

    85	
    86	### Spec edits
    87	
    88	+ Every cross-component contract (wire format, state machine, invariant) is defined in `spec/` first, then implemented in `reference/`. When they disagree, the spec wins and the impl gets a bug.
    89	+ Spec chapters use RFC 2119 language: MUST, SHOULD, MAY.
    90	+ Spec versions are frozen once stable (`spec/v0/` is a single lineage; breaking changes go to `spec/v1/`).
    91	+ Schema changes must update the spec prose, the JSON Schema file, and (from Phase 3 onward) the Pydantic bindings in lockstep. CI will enforce schema ↔ model parity once both exist.
    92	
    93	### Reference implementation
    94	
    95	+ Normal code-review discipline.
    96	+ Normative behavior is tested against the conformance suite (once it exists in Phase 11).
    97	+ The reference impl is explicitly not a monopoly on correctness. A third-party implementation that passes conformance is equally valid.
    98	
    99	### Adding a new service or package with its own `tests/` directory
   100	
   101	Pytest's default `prepend` import mode names test modules by file basename, so two `tests/test_app.py` files in different packages collide on collection (`import file mismatch`). The repo's tests-dir layout is mixed — some packages (e.g. [`eden-contracts/tests/`](reference/packages/eden-contracts/tests/)) have `__init__.py` and use `from .conftest import …`; most service test dirs (e.g. [`web-ui/tests/`](reference/services/web-ui/tests/)) have no `__init__.py` and use `from conftest import …`.
   102	
   103	When adding a new tests directory:
   104	
   105	+ Use a basename that is unique across the whole `testpaths` set in [`pyproject.toml`](pyproject.toml). Grep `find reference -name 'test_*.py' -exec basename {} \;` to verify before merging.
   106	+ Do **not** add `__init__.py` to a service tests dir while another tests dir already has one — both directories become the `tests` package and the second conftest collides on `tests.conftest` import/registration. Under default `prepend` mode this surfaces as `ImportPathMismatchError`; under `importlib` mode it surfaces as `Plugin already registered under a different name`.
   107	+ Switching the whole repo to `--import-mode=importlib` looks tempting but is **not** a clean fix today: the existing eden-contracts tests use relative `.conftest` imports that depend on the `__init__.py` package layout, and importlib mode keeps that collision while removing the sys.path hack the rest of the suite relies on.
   108	
   109	If the constraint becomes load-bearing later (Phase 10 will add several more service test dirs), the cleanest fix is a three-step lockstep: rename each tests dir to a unique package name (e.g. `web_ui_tests/` instead of `tests/`), switch every bare `from conftest import …` to a package-relative `from .conftest import …`, and add `--import-mode=importlib` to pytest's `addopts`. A mechanical basename-uniqueness check in CI is also a sensible follow-up — AGENTS-only guidance is weaker than a guardrail.

    87	## Design
    88	
    89	### A. Test layout
    90	
    91	Two new files under `reference/compose/healthcheck/`:
    92	
    93	- **`e2e.sh`** — bash wrapper. Owns: tooling preflight, setup-experiment
    94	  invocation, staged `compose up -d`, dispatch to the python driver,
    95	  post-run event-log assertions via `jq`, termination drill, teardown.
    96	- **`e2e_drive.py`** — python driver. Owns: web UI sign-in, CSRF
    97	  extraction, planner-walkthrough claim/submit, admin-reclaim POST,
    98	  exit non-zero with diagnostic on assertion failure.
    99	
   100	The split is deliberate: bash is the natural shape for `compose` /
   101	`docker inspect` / `jq` plumbing; python is the natural shape for
   102	cookie-aware HTTP and form-CSRF round-tripping. Mirroring the existing
   103	smoke shells keeps the CI job shape uniform.
   104	
   105	### B. Bring-up sequence — the race-against-planner-host fix
   106	
   107	**Codex round-0 finding (blocker, addressed):** the scripted planner-host
   108	drains pending plan tasks in a tight loop on startup
   109	([`workers.py`](../../reference/packages/eden-dispatch/src/eden_dispatch/workers.py)).
   110	If the stack starts with planner-host running and the orchestrator
   111	seeds 4 plan tasks via `--plan-tasks 4`, the planner-host can claim
   112	all 4 before the python driver finishes signing in. So a "stop
   113	planner-host after up" pattern is fundamentally racy — the bad race
   114	is during initial startup, not steady state.
   115	
   116	**Fix:** start the stack *without* planner-host, run the UI
   117	walkthroughs against the seeded-but-unclaimed plan tasks while no
   118	planner-host is competing, then bring planner-host online:
   119	
   120	```bash
   121	# Stage 1: bring up everything we actually USE in the drill, minus
   122	# planner-host. We deliberately omit gitea: it has a 30s healthcheck
   123	# start_period and is not consumed by 10e (Gitea-as-remote is the
   124	# 10d follow-up sub-chunk, deferred). The orchestrator has no
   125	# healthcheck and exits after 30 × 1s of no progress, so we must NOT
   126	# wait on a 30s+ healthcheck during stage-1 --wait, or the
   127	# orchestrator can quiesce and exit 0 mid-wait — `compose up --wait`
   128	# treats an exited service during the wait window as failure
   129	# (codex round-1 finding).
   130	#
   131	# Also omit planner-host (the whole reason for staging).
   132	#
   133	# What we DO wait on: postgres (5s start_period), task-store-server
   134	# (10s), web-ui (15s). Plus blob-init completes successfully in ~2s.
   135	# Worst-case wait is ~40s — well under 30s of orchestrator quiescence
   136	# from when it actually starts polling, because the orchestrator only
   137	# starts after task-store-server is healthy.
   138	docker compose -f compose.yaml --env-file "$ENV_FILE" up -d --wait \
   139	    --wait-timeout 120 \
   140	    postgres blob-init \
   141	    task-store-server orchestrator \
   142	    implementer-host evaluator-host web-ui
   143	
   144	# Sanity: the orchestrator must still be running by the time --wait
   145	# returns. If it exited early (quiesced because we took too long,
   146	# or crashed), the drill cannot proceed.
   147	status="$(docker inspect --format '{{.State.Status}}' eden-orchestrator)"
   148	test "$status" = "running" || {
   149	    echo "orchestrator already exited at end of stage 1 (status=$status)" >&2
   150	    docker compose -f compose.yaml --env-file "$ENV_FILE" \
   151	        logs --tail 60 orchestrator >&2
   152	    exit 1
   153	}
   154	
   155	# orchestrator seeded plan-0001..plan-0004 at startup. None claimed.
   156	# Run python driver — claims plan-0001 (submit) and plan-0002
   157	# (admin-reclaim drill). The submit-then-finalize edge produces
   158	# orchestrator dispatch work (the implement task for plan-0001's
   159	# proposal becomes claimable), which counts as orchestrator-side
   160	# progress and resets the quiescence counter. (The driver's claim
   161	# events alone don't reset quiescence — orchestrator "progress" is
   162	# its own finalize/dispatch/integrate work, not external claims.)
   163	
   164	python3 healthcheck/e2e_drive.py
   165	
   166	# Stage 2: bring planner-host online. It now picks up plan-0002
   167	# (returned to pending by admin-reclaim), plan-0003, and plan-0004.
   168	# Gitea is still down — not started, not used.
   169	docker compose -f compose.yaml --env-file "$ENV_FILE" up -d \
   170	    --wait --wait-timeout 60 planner-host
   171	```
   172	
   173	**Note on `EDEN_PLAN_TASKS=4`:** the orchestrator's `--plan-tasks` flag
   174	defaults to 3 in the fixture. We override to 4 in `.env` so the four
   175	deterministic IDs `plan-0001..plan-0004` are seeded. The python driver
   176	uses two specific IDs (`plan-0001` for the submit walkthrough,
   177	`plan-0002` for the admin-reclaim drill); the other two go through the
   178	headless planner-host on the success path.
   179	
   180	```bash
   181	# After setup-experiment.sh writes .env, override:
   182	sed -i.bak 's/^EDEN_PLAN_TASKS=.*/EDEN_PLAN_TASKS=4/' "$ENV_FILE"
   183	rm -f "${ENV_FILE}.bak"
   184	```
   185	
   186	The `sed -i.bak` form is GNU/BSD-portable (BSD `sed -i ''` rejects
   187	the bare `-i`; GNU `sed -i.bak` accepts it identically).
   188	
   189	**Confirmed plan-task ID shape** ([`orchestrator/cli.py:116`](../../reference/services/orchestrator/src/eden_orchestrator/cli.py)):
   190	numeric `--plan-tasks N` expands to `plan-0001..plan-{N:04d}`. So
   191	with `N=4` we have exactly `plan-0001`, `plan-0002`, `plan-0003`,
   192	`plan-0004`.

   213	    headers={"content-type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded"},

   215	)

   217	
   218	# 4. GET /planner/plan-0001/draft — re-scrape CSRF (form re-rendered)

   223	# 5. POST /planner/plan-0001/submit — note: returns 200 with rendered

   227	    ("status", "success"),
   228	    ("slug", "e2e-feat"),
   229	    ("priority", "1.0"),
   230	    ("parent_commits", base_sha),  # from $EDEN_BASE_COMMIT_SHA in env

   289	### E. Event-log read-back
   290	
   291	`task-store-server` is internal-only — no published host port. The
   292	python driver cannot read events directly; the post-walkthrough
   293	`task.reclaimed` assertion runs from `e2e.sh` via `compose exec`,
   294	exactly the way `smoke.sh` already does:
   295	
   296	```bash
   297	EVENTS_JSON="$(
   298	    docker compose -f compose.yaml --env-file "$ENV_FILE" \
   299	        exec -T task-store-server \
   300	        curl -fsS \
   301	            -H "Authorization: Bearer ${EDEN_SHARED_TOKEN}" \
   302	            -H "X-Eden-Experiment-Id: ${EXPERIMENT_ID}" \
   303	            "http://localhost:8080/v0/experiments/${EXPERIMENT_ID}/events"
   304	)"
   305	
   306	RECLAIMED_OPERATOR="$(
   307	    echo "$EVENTS_JSON" \
   308	        | jq '(.events // .) | [.[] | select(
   309	              .type == "task.reclaimed"
   310	              and .data.cause == "operator"
   311	            )] | length'
   312	)"
   313	test "$RECLAIMED_OPERATOR" -ge 1
   314	```

   339	### H. Termination drill — full container set
   340	
   341	**Codex round-0 finding (medium, addressed):** the original drill
   342	narrowed to `eden-*` containers, missing `eden-postgres`, `eden-gitea`,
   343	and `eden-blob-init`. Also: `eden-repo-init` ran via
   344	`compose run --rm --no-deps` and is gone by termination time
   345	([`setup-experiment.sh:251`](../../reference/scripts/setup-experiment/setup-experiment.sh)).
   346	
   347	The drill operates over the actual `compose ps -a` output:
   348	
   349	```bash
   350	echo "--- termination drill: stop with 10s budget ---"
   351	docker compose -f compose.yaml --env-file "$ENV_FILE" stop --timeout 10
   352	
   353	# Iterate every container compose still knows about, regardless of
   354	# name pattern. This naturally covers postgres, gitea, blob-init,
   355	# task-store-server, orchestrator, planner-host, implementer-host,
   356	# evaluator-host, and web-ui. eden-repo-init is gone (--rm) so it
   357	# does not appear.
   358	for name in $(
   359	    docker compose -f compose.yaml --env-file "$ENV_FILE" ps -a \
   360	        --format '{{.Name}}'
   361	); do
   362	    status="$(docker inspect --format '{{.State.Status}}' "$name")"
   363	    code="$(docker inspect --format '{{.State.ExitCode}}' "$name")"
   364	    test "$status" = "exited" || {
   365	        echo "$name still in status $status after stop --timeout 10" >&2
   366	        exit 1
   367	    }
   368	    test "$code" != "137" || {
   369	        echo "$name was SIGKILLed (exit code 137)" >&2
   370	        docker compose -f compose.yaml --env-file "$ENV_FILE" \
   371	            logs --tail 30 "$name" >&2
   372	        exit 1
   373	    }
   374	    # blob-init is a one-shot that already exited 0 during bring-up;
   375	    # this is just a paranoia check, not a status-change.
   376	done
   377	```
   378	
   379	**Why `!= 137` instead of `== 0`:**
   380	[`reference/services/_common/`](../../reference/services/_common/)'s
   381	SIGTERM handler exits the EDEN python services cleanly with code 0
   382	(verified by codex against
   383	[`web-ui cli.py:155`](../../reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/cli.py),
   384	[`task-store cli.py:171`](../../reference/services/task-store-server/src/eden_task_store_server/cli.py),
   385	[`planner cli.py:109`](../../reference/services/planner/src/eden_planner_host/cli.py),
   386	[`implementer cli.py:85`](../../reference/services/implementer/src/eden_implementer_host/cli.py),
   387	[`evaluator cli.py:90`](../../reference/services/evaluator/src/eden_evaluator_host/cli.py)).
   388	But postgres / gitea use external images we don't control; if a future
   389	upstream tweak changes their default SIGTERM handler to default-handler
   390	(producing 143), `== 0` would falsely fail. `!= 137` captures the only
   391	thing we actually care about: **the container did NOT need SIGKILL**.
   392	
   393	### I. Teardown
   394	
   395	`e2e.sh`'s `cleanup()` trap runs `docker compose down -v` on EXIT,
   396	exactly as smoke.sh does. The termination drill's `compose stop` does
   397	NOT call `down -v` itself — that would lose the volumes before the
   398	drill verifies anything. `down -v` is the trap's responsibility;
   399	`stop` is the drill's.

   434	### K. Failure-mode handling (revised)
   435

item.text:
**Findings**

1. Blocker: [ci.yml](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/.github/workflows/ci.yml:174) installs `httpx` with bare `pip` on `ubuntu-latest`, but the job never pins or activates a non-system Python. That is brittle at best and currently risky on GitHub-hosted Ubuntu: `ubuntu-latest` now maps to Ubuntu 24.04, GitHub’s runner image documents a system `Python 3.12.3` / `Pip 24.0`, and GitHub’s own runner-images issue tracker documents `pip install` failing there with PEP 668 unless `actions/setup-python` is used. Even if `pip` succeeds, [e2e.sh](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/compose/healthcheck/e2e.sh:27) later imports via `python3`, so `pip`/`python3` skew is another avoidable failure mode. This should be `actions/setup-python` plus `python3 -m pip install ...`.

2. Medium: [AGENTS.md](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/AGENTS.md:74) still lists “Comprehensive Compose end-to-end test (with web-ui walkthroughs)” under “Commands that will exist in later phases,” even though 10e is already marked complete at [AGENTS.md](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/AGENTS.md:19) and the real command row exists at [AGENTS.md](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/AGENTS.md:69). That’s stale repo guidance.

3. Medium: [e2e_drive.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/compose/healthcheck/e2e_drive.py:202) only uses `_fail()` for bad HTTP responses; raw `httpx` transport errors are uncaught. A connect timeout / refused connection / read timeout will dump a traceback instead of the intended concise diagnostic. That does not violate the happy path, but it weakens the failure-mode handling the script is otherwise aiming for.

**Assessment**

Against the intended plan, the implementation is otherwise solid. The staged bring-up in [e2e.sh](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/compose/healthcheck/e2e.sh:82), the planner/admin route assumptions in [planner.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/routes/planner.py:88), [admin.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/routes/admin.py:307), [planner_list.html](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/templates/planner_list.html:32), [planner_claim.html](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/templates/planner_claim.html:27), [admin_task_detail.html](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/templates/admin_task_detail.html:37), and [planner_submitted.html](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/templates/planner_submitted.html:4) all line up. I did not find scope creep or missing plan-promised implementation.

On the specific questions:
- Race correctness: the planner-host startup race is addressed correctly. The 30s `/planner/` poll is sensible. The “orchestrator must still be running” check is in the right place, though not mathematically airtight: if the driver stalls badly, the orchestrator could still quiesce after that check and before submit. I’d treat that as residual risk, not a blocker.
- Termination drill: the blob-init expectation is correct because [compose.yaml](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/compose/compose.yaml:72) defines a persistent one-shot container and stage 1 starts it at [e2e.sh](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/compose/healthcheck/e2e.sh:46). A restarting container would fail the drill, which is appropriate. One bash caveat: the process-substitution producer at [e2e.sh](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/compose/healthcheck/e2e.sh:200) is not protected by `pipefail`; if `docker compose ps -a` failed after partial output, the loop would not notice that directly.
- Driver robustness: CSRF scraping matches the current templates, session-cookie handling via `httpx.Client` is fine, and the admin reclaim `Location` equality matches the current redirect in [admin.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/routes/admin.py:331).
- Docs: [docs/roadmap.md](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/roadmap.md:201) is accurate. The only doc problem I found is the stale AGENTS “later phases” row. One minor note: the plan text itself has stale details in a couple places, but the shipped code/docs chose the correct side of those inconsistencies.

Overall: not ready to ship as-is because of the CI job’s Python install step. Fix that, and I would consider this ready.

CI-source references: [runner-images README](https://github.com/actions/runner-images), [Ubuntu 24.04 runner software list](https://github.com/actions/runner-images/blob/main/images/ubuntu/Ubuntu2404-Readme.md), [documented PEP 668 failure on runner-images](https://github.com/actions/runner-images/issues/10781), [setup-python hosted tool cache docs](https://github.com/actions/setup-python/blob/main/docs/advanced-usage.md).
